tables.name: current
tables.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE current
(
    ballotpedia_id      TEXT,
    bioguide_id         TEXT,
    birthday_bio        DATE,
    cspan_id            REAL,
    fec_id              TEXT,
    first_name          TEXT,
    gender_bio          TEXT,
    google_entity_id_id TEXT,
    govtrack_id         INTEGER,
    house_history_id    REAL,
    icpsr_id            REAL,
    last_name           TEXT,
    lis_id              TEXT,
    maplight_id         REAL,
    middle_name         TEXT,
    nickname_name       TEXT,
    official_full_name  TEXT,
    opensecrets_id      TEXT,
    religion_bio        TEXT,
    suffix_name         TEXT,
    thomas_id           INTEGER,
    votesmart_id        REAL,
    wikidata_id         TEXT,
    wikipedia_id        TEXT,
        primary key (bioguide_id, cspan_id)
)

bird_description.description: The ballotpedia.org page name for the person (spaces are given as spaces, not underscores).
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
if this value is null or empty, it means this legislator doesn't have account on ballotpedia.org

bird_description.description: The alphanumeric ID for this legislator
The legislator's birthday,
bird_description.value_description: in YYYY-MM-DD format.

bird_description.description: The numeric ID for this legislator on C-SPAN's video website,
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
if this value is null or empty, it means this legislator doesn't have account on C-SPAN's video website

bird_description.description: A list of IDs for this legislator in Federal Election Commission data.
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
if this value is null or empty, it means this legislator hasn't not been registered in Federal Election Commission data.

bird_description.description: first name of the legislator
gender of the legislator
google entity id
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
if this value is null or empty, it means this legislator are not google entities

bird_description.description: The numeric ID for this legislator on GovTrack.us
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
if this value is null or empty, it means this legislator doesn't have account on GovTrack.us

bird_description.description: The numeric ID for this legislator on http://history.house.gov/People/Search/
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
The ID is present only for members who have served in the U.S. House.

bird_description.semantic_name: interuniversity consortium for political and social research id
bird_description.description: The numeric ID for this legislator in Keith Poole's VoteView.com website, originally based on an ID system by the Interuniversity Consortium for Political and Social Research (stored as an integer).
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
if this value is null or empty, it means this legislator doesn't have account on VoteView.com

bird_description.description: last name of the legislator
The alphanumeric ID for this legislator found in Senate roll call votes
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
The ID is present only for members who attended in Senate roll call votes

bird_description.description: The numeric ID for this legislator on maplight.org
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
if this value is null or empty, it means this legislator doesn't have account on maplight.org

bird_description.description: the middle name of the legislator
nickname of the legislator
official full name
The alphanumeric ID for this legislator on OpenSecrets.org.
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
if this value is null or empty, it means this legislator doesn't have account on OpenSecrets.org.

bird_description.description: The legislator's religion.
suffix name
The numeric ID for this legislator on http://thomas.gov and http://beta.congress.gov.
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
if this value is null or empty, it means this legislator doesn't have account on both http://thomas.gov and http://beta.congress.gov.

bird_description.description: The numeric ID for this legislator on VoteSmart.org
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
if this value is null or empty, it means this legislator doesn't have account on VoteSmart.org

bird_description.description: the id for wikidata
The Wikipedia page name for the person
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
if a legislator has wikipedia id, it means he or she is famous or impact

tables.name: current-terms
tables.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE "current-terms"
(
    address            TEXT,
    bioguide           TEXT,
    caucus             TEXT,
    chamber            TEXT,
    class              REAL,
    contact_form       TEXT,
    district           REAL,
    end                TEXT,
    fax                TEXT,
    last               TEXT,
    name               TEXT,
    office             TEXT,
    party              TEXT,
    party_affiliations TEXT,
    phone              TEXT,
    relation           TEXT,
    rss_url            TEXT,
    start              TEXT,
    state              TEXT,
    state_rank         TEXT,
    title              TEXT,
    type               TEXT,
    url                TEXT,
    primary key (bioguide, end),
    foreign key (bioguide) references current(bioguide_id)
)

bird_description.description: the address of this legislator
The alphanumeric ID for this legislator
caucus
bird_description.value_description: For independents, the party that the legislator caucuses with, using the same values as the party field. Omitted if the legislator caucuses with the party indicated in the party field. When in doubt about the difference between the party and caucus fields, the party field is what displays after the legislator's name (i.e. "(D)") but the caucus field is what normally determines committee seniority. This field was added starting with terms for the 113th Congress.

bird_description.description: chamber
bird_description.value_description:
â¢ senate
â¢ house

bird_description.description: class
bird_description.value_description:
For senators, their election class (1, 2, or 3). 
commonsense evidence:
only senator has class, if the value is null or empty, it means this legislator is not senator.

bird_description.description: The website URL of the contact page on the legislator's official website
district
bird_description.value_description:
For representatives, the district number they are serving from. 
commonsense evidence:
if null or empty, they are not representatives.

bird_description.description: the end of the term
bird_description.value_description: end: The date the term ended (because the Congress ended or the legislator died or resigned, etc.). End dates follow the Constitutional end of a term. Since 1935, terms begin and end on January 3 at noon in odd-numbered years, and thus a term end date may also be a term start date. Prior to 1935, terms began on March 4 and ended either on March 3 or March 4. The end date is the last date on which the legislator served this term. Unlike the start date, whether Congress was in session or not does not affect the value of this field.

bird_description.description: The fax number of the legislator's Washington, D.C. office
bird_description.value_description: only valid if the term is current

bird_description.description: the last known number

bird_description.value_description: not useful

bird_description.description: office
bird_description.value_description: only valid if the term is current, otherwise the last known office

bird_description.description: The political party of the legislator.
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
If the legislator changed parties, this is the most recent party held during the term and party_affiliations will be set. Values are typically "Democrat", "Independent", or "Republican". The value typically matches the political party of the legislator on the ballot in his or her last election, although for state affiliate parties such as "Democratic Farmer Labor" we will use the national party name ("Democrat") instead to keep the values of this field normalized.

bird_description.description: This field is present if the legislator changed party or caucus affiliation during the term.
bird_description.value_description: The value is a list of time periods, with start and end dates, each of which has a party field and a caucus field if applicable, with the same meanings as the main party and caucus fields. The time periods cover the entire term, so the first start will match the term start, the last end will match the term end, and the last party (and caucus if present) will match the term party (and caucus).

bird_description.description: The phone number of the legislator's Washington, D.C. office
bird_description.value_description: only valid if the term is current, otherwise the last known number
not useful

bird_description.description: The URL to the official website's RSS feed
The date legislative service began: the date the legislator was sworn in, if known, or else the beginning of the legislator's term.
bird_description.value_description: Since 1935 regularly elected terms begin on January 3 at noon on odd-numbered years, but when Congress does not first meet on January 3, term start dates might reflect that swearing-in occurred on a later date. (Prior to 1935, terms began on March 4 of odd-numbered years, see here.)

bird_description.description: state code
bird_description.value_description:
commonsense evidence:
AK: Alaska 
AL: Alabama 
AR: Arkansas 
AZ: Arizona 
CA: California 
CO: Colorado 
CT: Connecticut 
DE: Delaware 
FL: Florida 
GA: Georgia 
HI: Hawaii 
IA: Iowa 
ID: Idaho 
IL: Illinois 
IN: Indiana 
KS: Kansas 
KY: Kentucky 
LA: Louisiana 
MA: Massachusetts 
MD: Maryland 
ME: Maine 
MI: Michigan 
MN: Minnesota 
MO: Missouri 
MS: Mississippi 
MT: Montana 
NC: North Carolina 
ND: North Dakota 
NE: Nebraska 
NH: New Hampshire 
NJ: New Jersey
9 divisions of states in us: (please mention)
https://www2.census.gov/geo/pdfs/maps-data/maps/reference/us_regdiv.pdf

bird_description.description: whether they are the "junior" or "senior" senator
bird_description.value_description:
only valid if the term is current, otherwise the senator's rank at the time the term ended
commonsense evidence:
only senator has this value

bird_description.description: title of the legislator
The type of the term.
bird_description.value_description: Either "sen" for senators or "rep" for representatives and delegates to the House

bird_description.description: The official website URL of the legislator
bird_description.value_description: only valid if the term is current

tables.name: social-media
tables.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE "social-media"
(
    bioguide     TEXT
            primary key,
    facebook     TEXT,
    facebook_id  REAL,
    govtrack     REAL,
    instagram    TEXT,
    instagram_id REAL,
    thomas       INTEGER,
    twitter      TEXT,
    twitter_id   REAL,
    youtube      TEXT,
    youtube_id   TEXT,
    foreign key (bioguide) references current(bioguide_id)
)

bird_description.description: The unique alphanumeric ID for this legislator
The username of the current official Facebook presence of the legislator.
The numeric ID of the current official Facebook presence of the legislator.
The numeric ID for this legislator on GovTrack.us
The current official Instagram handle of the legislator.
The numeric ID of the current official Instagram handle of the legislator.
The numeric ID for this legislator on http://thomas.gov and http://beta.congress.gov.
The current official Twitter handle of the legislator.
The numeric ID of the current official twitter handle of the legislator.
The current official YouTube username of the legislator.
The current official YouTube channel ID of the legislator.
